content.text: I'll start by reading the key reference files to understand the existing formats, then work through each task systematically.

todos.content: Read existing ontology, aliases, and prompts files
Create financial_ontology.yaml with >=12 entity types and >=14 relationship types
Create financial_aliases.yaml with >=50 bilingual aliases
Create Basel III corpus (Pilar 1, 2, 3) in Markdown
Create LGPD corpus (65 articles) in Markdown
Create BCB resolutions corpus in Markdown
Document pipeline execution with financial ontology
Document Neo4j KG with >=500 triples
Evaluate sample of 50 triples with precision >=80%
Document pipeline metrics (chunks, triples, entities, cost)
Execute and document 10 financial queries test

content.content: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable

file.content:
# =============================================================================
# GraphRAG PanelBox - Ontology Definition
# =============================================================================
# This file defines the knowledge graph schema for the PanelBox library.
# All entity types, relationship types, and their properties are specified here.
# =============================================================================

version: "1.0"
name: "PanelBox GraphRAG Ontology"
description: "Knowledge graph schema for PanelBox panel data econometrics library"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entity Types (Node Labels in Neo4j)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
entity_types:

  Estimator:
    description: "Econometric model class in PanelBox"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Class name (e.g., FixedEffects, DifferenceGMM)"
      module_path:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Python module path (e.g., panelbox.models.static.fixed_effects)"
      class_name:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Python class name"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Brief description of the estimator"
      estimation_method:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Estimation approach (OLS, MLE, GMM, QR, etc.)"
      usage_example:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Python code snippet showing basic usage"
      assumptions:
        type: list
        required: false
        description: "Statistical assumptions for valid inference"
      min_T:
        type: integer
        required: false
        description: "Minimum number of time periods required"
      min_N:
        type: integer
        required: false
        description: "Minimum number of entities recommended"
      supports_unbalanced:
        type: boolean
        required: false
        description: "Whether unbalanced panels are supported"

  DiagnosticTest:
    description: "Statistical test for model validation or diagnostics"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Test name (e.g., HausmanTest, HansenJTest)"
      module_path:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Python module path"
      function_name:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Python function or class name"
      null_hypothesis:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "H0 statement"
      reject_means:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Interpretation when H0 is rejected"
      distribution:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Test statistic distribution (Chi-squared, F, etc.)"
      interpretation_guide:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Practical interpretation guidelines"
      usage_example:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Python code snippet"

  Parameter:
    description: "Function argument or configuration option"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Parameter name (e.g., instruments, lags)"
      type:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Python type hint"
      default:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Default value"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "What the parameter controls"
      valid_values:
        type: list
        required: false
        description: "List of accepted values (for enum-like params)"

  StatisticalConcept:
    description: "Theoretical concept in econometrics"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Concept name (e.g., Endogeneity, Heteroskedasticity)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Plain-language explanation"
      formal_definition:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Mathematical/formal definition"
      implications:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "What happens if this is present/violated"

  Literature:
    description: "Academic reference or paper"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Citation (e.g., Hausman (1978))"
      full_citation:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Full bibliographic reference"
      year:
        type: integer
        required: false
        description: "Publication year"
      key_contribution:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Main contribution of the paper"

  ModelFamily:
    description: "Grouping of related estimators"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Family name (e.g., StaticModels, GMM)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "What this family covers"
      module_prefix:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Common module prefix (e.g., panelbox.models.static)"

  StandardError:
    description: "Type of robust standard error"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "SE type name (e.g., ClusteredSE, DriscollKraay)"
      class_name:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Python class name"
      module_path:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Python module path"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "When to use this type of SE"
      cov_type_param:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Value for cov_type parameter"

  Dataset:
    description: "Built-in dataset in PanelBox"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Dataset identifier (e.g., grunfeld, abdata)"
      loader_function:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Function to load (e.g., load_grunfeld)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Dataset description"
      n_entities:
        type: integer
        required: false
        description: "Number of cross-sectional units"
      n_periods:
        type: integer
        required: false
        description: "Number of time periods"
      variables:
        type: list
        required: false
        description: "Key variables in the dataset"

  CodePattern:
    description: "Common usage pattern or recipe"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Pattern name (e.g., fit_and_validate, compare_fe_re)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "What this pattern demonstrates"
      code:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Python code implementing the pattern"

  ResultMetric:
    description: "Output metric from model estimation"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Metric name (e.g., R_squared, AIC)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "What the metric measures"
      interpretation:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "How to interpret the value"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Relationship Types (Edge Types in Neo4j)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
relationship_types:

  BELONGS_TO:
    description: "Estimator belongs to a model family"
    source: Estimator
    target: ModelFamily

  INHERITS_FROM:
    description: "Estimator inherits from a base class"
    source: Estimator
    target: Estimator

  VALIDATES:
    description: "Diagnostic test validates an estimator"
    source: DiagnosticTest
    target: Estimator
    properties:
      mandatory:
        type: boolean
        description: "Whether this validation is mandatory"
      order:
        type: integer
        description: "Suggested order of execution"

  REQUIRES:
    description: "Estimator requires a parameter"
    source: Estimator
    target: Parameter
    properties:
      is_optional:
        type: boolean
        description: "Whether the parameter is optional"

  PRODUCES:
    description: "Estimator produces a result metric"
    source: Estimator
    target: ResultMetric

  ADDRESSES:
    description: "Estimator addresses a statistical concept"
    source: Estimator
    target: StatisticalConcept

  TESTS_FOR:
    description: "Diagnostic test checks for a statistical concept"
    source: DiagnosticTest
    target: StatisticalConcept

  BASED_ON:
    description: "Implementation is based on academic literature"
    source: [Estimator, DiagnosticTest]
    target: Literature

  ALTERNATIVE_TO:
    description: "Estimator is an alternative to another"
    source: Estimator
    target: Estimator
    properties:
      comparison_note:
        type: string
        description: "Key difference between the two"

  PRECONDITION:
    description: "Test should be run before using an estimator"
    source: DiagnosticTest
    target: Estimator

  SUPPORTS_SE:
    description: "Estimator supports a type of standard error"
    source: Estimator
    target: StandardError

  DEMONSTRATED_IN:
    description: "Estimator is demonstrated in a dataset"
    source: Estimator
    target: Dataset

  IMPLEMENTED_BY:
    description: "Code pattern implements usage of an estimator"
    source: CodePattern
    target: Estimator

  SUCCEEDS:
    description: "Test should be run after another test"
    source: DiagnosticTest
    target: DiagnosticTest

1→# =============================================================================
     2→# GraphRAG PanelBox - Entity Aliases
     3→# =============================================================================
     4→# Maps common names, abbreviations, and variations to canonical entity names.
     5→# Used during entity resolution in the ingestion pipeline.
     6→# =============================================================================
     7→
     8→version: "1.0"
     9→
    10→estimator_aliases:
    11→  # Static Models
    12→  "FE": "FixedEffects"
    13→  "Fixed Effects": "FixedEffects"
    14→  "Within Estimator": "FixedEffects"
    15→  "Within": "FixedEffects"
    16→  "LSDV": "FixedEffects"
    17→  "RE": "RandomEffects"
    18→  "Random Effects": "RandomEffects"
    19→  "GLS": "RandomEffects"
    20→  "Pooled OLS": "PooledOLS"
    21→  "POLS": "PooledOLS"
    22→  "Between": "BetweenEstimator"
    23→  "FD": "FirstDifferenceEstimator"
    24→  "First Difference": "FirstDifferenceEstimator"
    25→
    26→  # GMM
    27→  "Arellano-Bond": "DifferenceGMM"
    28→  "Arellano Bond": "DifferenceGMM"
    29→  "AB GMM": "DifferenceGMM"
    30→  "Difference GMM": "DifferenceGMM"
    31→  "Blundell-Bond": "SystemGMM"
    32→  "Blundell Bond": "SystemGMM"
    33→  "BB GMM": "SystemGMM"
    34→  "System GMM": "SystemGMM"
    35→  "CUE-GMM": "ContinuousUpdatedGMM"
    36→  "CUE GMM": "ContinuousUpdatedGMM"
    37→
    38→  # Spatial
    39→  "SAR": "SpatialLag"
    40→  "Spatial Lag": "SpatialLag"
    41→  "Spatial Autoregressive": "SpatialLag"
    42→  "SEM": "SpatialError"
    43→  "Spatial Error": "SpatialError"
    44→  "SDM": "SpatialDurbin"
    45→  "Spatial Durbin": "SpatialDurbin"
    46→  "GNS": "GeneralNestingSpatial"
    47→
    48→  # Count
    49→  "Poisson FE": "PoissonFixedEffects"
    50→  "Poisson RE": "RandomEffectsPoisson"
    51→  "NegBin": "NegativeBinomial"
    52→  "Negative Binomial": "NegativeBinomial"
    53→  "ZIP": "ZeroInflatedPoisson"
    54→  "ZINB": "ZeroInflatedNegativeBinomial"
    55→  "Gravity Model": "PPML"
    56→
    57→  # Discrete
    58→  "Logit": "PooledLogit"
    59→  "Probit": "PooledProbit"
    60→  "FE Logit": "FixedEffectsLogit"
    61→  "RE Probit": "RandomEffectsProbit"
    62→
    63→  # Frontier
    64→  "SFA": "StochasticFrontier"
    65→  "Stochastic Frontier": "StochasticFrontier"
    66→  "Four Component": "FourComponentSFA"
    67→
    68→  # VAR
    69→  "Panel VAR": "PanelVAR"
    70→  "PVAR": "PanelVAR"
    71→  "VECM": "PanelVECM"
    72→
    73→  # Other
    74→  "Tobit": "PooledTobit"
    75→  "Heckman": "PanelHeckman"
    76→  "2SLS": "PanelIV"
    77→  "IV": "PanelIV"
    78→

# =============================================================================
# GraphRAG PanelBox - Entity Aliases
# =============================================================================
# Maps common names, abbreviations, and variations to canonical entity names.
# Used during entity resolution in the ingestion pipeline.
# =============================================================================

version: "1.0"

estimator_aliases:
  # Static Models
  "FE": "FixedEffects"
  "Fixed Effects": "FixedEffects"
  "Within Estimator": "FixedEffects"
  "Within": "FixedEffects"
  "LSDV": "FixedEffects"
  "RE": "RandomEffects"
  "Random Effects": "RandomEffects"
  "GLS": "RandomEffects"
  "Pooled OLS": "PooledOLS"
  "POLS": "PooledOLS"
  "Between": "BetweenEstimator"
  "FD": "FirstDifferenceEstimator"
  "First Difference": "FirstDifferenceEstimator"

  # GMM
  "Arellano-Bond": "DifferenceGMM"
  "Arellano Bond": "DifferenceGMM"
  "AB GMM": "DifferenceGMM"
  "Difference GMM": "DifferenceGMM"
  "Blundell-Bond": "SystemGMM"
  "Blundell Bond": "SystemGMM"
  "BB GMM": "SystemGMM"
  "System GMM": "SystemGMM"
  "CUE-GMM": "ContinuousUpdatedGMM"
  "CUE GMM": "ContinuousUpdatedGMM"

  # Spatial
  "SAR": "SpatialLag"
  "Spatial Lag": "SpatialLag"
  "Spatial Autoregressive": "SpatialLag"
  "SEM": "SpatialError"
  "Spatial Error": "SpatialError"
  "SDM": "SpatialDurbin"
  "Spatial Durbin": "SpatialDurbin"
  "GNS": "GeneralNestingSpatial"

  # Count
  "Poisson FE": "PoissonFixedEffects"
  "Poisson RE": "RandomEffectsPoisson"
  "NegBin": "NegativeBinomial"
  "Negative Binomial": "NegativeBinomial"
  "ZIP": "ZeroInflatedPoisson"
  "ZINB": "ZeroInflatedNegativeBinomial"
  "Gravity Model": "PPML"

  # Discrete
  "Logit": "PooledLogit"
  "Probit": "PooledProbit"
  "FE Logit": "FixedEffectsLogit"
  "RE Probit": "RandomEffectsProbit"

  # Frontier
  "SFA": "StochasticFrontier"
  "Stochastic Frontier": "StochasticFrontier"
  "Four Component": "FourComponentSFA"

  # VAR
  "Panel VAR": "PanelVAR"
  "PVAR": "PanelVAR"
  "VECM": "PanelVECM"

  # Other
  "Tobit": "PooledTobit"
  "Heckman": "PanelHeckman"
  "2SLS": "PanelIV"
  "IV": "PanelIV"

diagnostic_test_aliases:
  "Hausman": "HausmanTest"
  "Hausman test": "HausmanTest"
  "Hansen J": "HansenJTest"
  "Hansen J-test": "HansenJTest"
  "Hansen test": "HansenJTest"
  "Sargan": "SarganTest"
  "Sargan test": "SarganTest"
  "AR(1) test": "AR1Test"
  "AR(2) test": "AR2Test"

  "Moran's I": "MoranITest"

  "BP test": "BreuschPaganTest"
  "Modified Wald": "ModifiedWaldTest"
  "White test": "WhiteTest"
  "Wooldridge AR": "WooldridgeARTest"
  "Wooldridge test": "WooldridgeARTest"
  "Pesaran CD": "PesaranCDTest"
  "LLC": "LLCTest"
  "IPS": "IPSTest"
  "Mundlak": "MundlakTest"
  "Chow": "ChowTest"
  "RESET": "RESETTest"

concept_aliases:
  "endogeneity": "Endogeneity"
  "endogenous": "Endogeneity"

  "heteroskedasticity": "Heteroskedasticity"

1→"""Extraction prompt templates for triple extraction via Claude API.
     2→
     3→Defines the system prompt with ontology constraints, user template
     4→for chunk content, and few-shot examples for each source type.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→# --- Ontology Definition ---
     8→
     9→ENTITY_TYPES = [
    10→    "Estimator",
    11→    "DiagnosticTest",
    12→    "Parameter",
    13→    "StatisticalConcept",
    14→    "Literature",
    15→    "ModelFamily",
    16→    "StandardError",
    17→    "Dataset",
    18→    "CodePattern",
    19→    "ResultMetric",
    20→]
    21→
    22→RELATION_TYPES = [
    23→    "BELONGS_TO",
    24→    "INHERITS_FROM",
    25→    "VALIDATES",
    26→    "REQUIRES",
    27→    "PRODUCES",
    28→    "ADDRESSES",
    29→    "TESTS_FOR",
    30→    "BASED_ON",
    31→    "ALTERNATIVE_TO",
    32→    "PRECONDITION",
    33→    "SUPPORTS_SE",
    34→    "DEMONSTRATED_IN",
    35→    "IMPLEMENTED_BY",
    36→    "SUCCEEDS",
    37→]
    38→
    39→# --- System Prompt ---
    40→
    41→EXTRACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a knowledge graph extraction engine for the PanelBox econometrics library.
    42→
    43→Your task is to extract (subject, predicate, object) triples from text chunks.
    44→Each triple represents a relationship between two entities in the PanelBox domain.
    45→
    46→## Valid Entity Types
    47→{entity_types}
    48→
    49→## Valid Relation Types
    50→{relation_types}
    51→
    52→## Rules
    53→1. Only use entity types and relation types from the lists above.
    54→2. Entity names should be PascalCase for classes (e.g., FixedEffects) and snake_case for parameters/concepts.
    55→3. Each triple must have a confidence score between 0.0 and 1.0.
    56→4. Include evidence: a short quote or reference from the source text.
    57→5. Do NOT invent entities that are not mentioned or clearly implied in the text.
    58→6. Prefer specific entity names over generic ones (e.g., "FixedEffects" not "model").
    59→7. A single chunk may produce 0 to 15 triples.
    60→
    61→## Output Format
    62→Respond with a JSON object containing a "triples" array. Each triple has:
    63→- subject: {{"name": str, "type": str}}
    64→- predicate: str (one of the valid relation types)
    65→- object: {{"name": str, "type": str}}
    66→- confidence: float (0.0 to 1.0)
    67→- evidence: str (short excerpt from source)
    68→
    69→If no triples can be extracted, return {{"triples": []}}.
    70→""".strip()
    71→
    72→# --- User Template ---
    73→
    74→EXTRACTION_USER_TEMPLATE = """Extract knowledge triples from this {chunk_type} chunk.
    75→
    76→Source: {source_file}
    77→Context: {context}
    78→
    79→---
    80→{content}
    81→---
    82→
    83→Return a JSON object with a "triples" array. Only use valid entity and relation types.""".strip()
    84→
    85→# --- Few-Shot Examples ---
    86→
    87→FEW_SHOT_EXAMPLES = [
    88→    {
    89→        "description": "Python class definition",
    90→        "chunk_type": "python_class",
    91→        "content": '''class FixedEffects(PanelEstimator):
    92→    """Fixed Effects (Within) estimator for panel data.
    93→
    94→    Uses the within transformation to eliminate entity-specific
    95→    fixed effects. Supports robust and clustered standard errors.
    96→
    97→    Based on Wooldridge (2002), Chapter 10.
    98→    """
    99→
   100→    def __init__(self, formula, data, entity_col, time_col,
   101→                 cov_type="robust"):
   102→        super().__init__(formula, data, entity_col, time_col)
   103→        self.cov_type = cov_type
   104→
   105→    def fit(self):
   106→        """Fit the model using within transformation."""
   107→        ...''',
   108→        "expected_output": {
   109→            "triples": [
   110→                {
   111→                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
   112→                    "predicate": "BELONGS_TO",
   113→                    "object": {"name": "StaticModels", "type": "ModelFamily"},
   114→                    "confidence": 0.90,
   115→                    "evidence": "class FixedEffects(PanelEstimator)"
   116→                },
   117→                {
   118→                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
   119→                    "predicate": "INHERITS_FROM",
   120→                    "object": {"name": "PanelEstimator", "type": "Estimator"},
   121→                    "confidence": 0.99,
   122→                    "evidence": "class FixedEffects(PanelEstimator)"
   123→                },
   124→                {
   125→                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
   126→                    "predicate": "SUPPORTS_SE",
   127→                    "object": {"name": "robust", "type": "StandardError"},
   128→                    "confidence": 0.95,
   129→                    "evidence": "Supports robust and clustered standard errors"
   130→                },
   131→                {
   132→                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
   133→                    "predicate": "SUPPORTS_SE",
   134→                    "object": {"name": "clustered", "type": "StandardError"},
   135→                    "confidence": 0.95,
   136→                    "evidence": "Supports robust and clustered standard errors"
   137→                },
   138→                {
   139→                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
   140→                    "predicate": "BASED_ON",
   141→                    "object": {"name": "Wooldridge2002", "type": "Literature"},
   142→                    "confidence": 0.95,
   143→                    "evidence": "Based on Wooldridge (2002), Chapter 10"
   144→                },
   145→                {
   146→                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
   147→                    "predicate": "ADDRESSES",
   148→                    "object": {"name": "entity_fixed_effects", "type": "StatisticalConcept"},
   149→                    "confidence": 0.90,
   150→                    "evidence": "eliminate entity-specific fixed effects"
   151→                },
   152→            ]
   153→        },
   154→    },
   155→    {
   156→        "description": "Markdown documentation section",
   157→        "chunk_type": "markdown_section",
   158→        "content": '''## Hausman Test
   159→
   160→The Hausman test compares Fixed Effects and Random Effects estimators
   161→to determine whether the individual effects are correlated with the
   162→regressors. Under the null hypothesis (RE is consistent), both
   163→estimators are consistent, but FE is inefficient.
   164→
   165→### Usage
   166→
   167→```python
   168→from panelbox.validation import hausman_test
   169→result = hausman_test(fe_result, re_result)
   170→print(result.statistic, result.p_value)
   171→```
   172→
   173→If p-value < 0.05, prefer Fixed Effects.''',
   174→        "expected_output": {
   175→            "triples": [
   176→                {
   177→                    "subject": {"name": "hausman_test", "type": "DiagnosticTest"},
   178→                    "predicate": "VALIDATES",
   179→                    "object": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
   180→                    "confidence": 0.95,
   181→                    "evidence": "compares Fixed Effects and Random Effects estimators"
   182→                },
   183→                {
   184→                    "subject": {"name": "hausman_test", "type": "DiagnosticTest"},
   185→                    "predicate": "VALIDATES",
   186→                    "object": {"name": "RandomEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
   187→                    "confidence": 0.95,
   188→                    "evidence": "compares Fixed Effects and Random Effects estimators"
   189→                },
   190→                {
   191→                    "subject": {"name": "hausman_test", "type": "DiagnosticTest"},
   192→                    "predicate": "TESTS_FOR",
   193→                    "object": {"name": "endogeneity", "type": "StatisticalConcept"},
   194→                    "confidence": 0.85,
   195→                    "evidence": "individual effects are correlated with the regressors"
   196→                },
   197→                {
   198→                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
   199→                    "predicate": "ALTERNATIVE_TO",
   200→                    "object": {"name": "RandomEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
   201→                    "confidence": 0.90,
   202→                    "evidence": "compares Fixed Effects and Random Effects"
   203→                },
   204→                {
   205→                    "subject": {"name": "hausman_test", "type": "DiagnosticTest"},
   206→                    "predicate": "PRODUCES",
   207→                    "object": {"name": "p_value", "type": "ResultMetric"},
   208→                    "confidence": 0.90,
   209→                    "evidence": "result.statistic, result.p_value"
   210→                },
   211→            ]
   212→        },
   213→    },
   214→    {
   215→        "description": "Notebook cell group",
   216→        "chunk_type": "notebook_cell_group",
   217→        "content": '''## Estimating with the Grunfeld Dataset
   218→
   219→We demonstrate the Fixed Effects estimator using the classic
   220→Grunfeld (1958) investment dataset.
   221→
   222→```python
   223→from panelbox.datasets import load_grunfeld
   224→from panelbox import FixedEffects
   225→
   226→data = load_grunfeld()
   227→model = FixedEffects("invest ~ value + capital", data, "firm", "year")
   228→result = model.fit(cov_type="clustered")
   229→print(f"R-squared (within): {result.r2_within:.4f}")
   230→```''',
   231→        "expected_output": {
   232→            "triples": [
   233→                {
   234→                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
   235→                    "predicate": "DEMONSTRATED_IN",
   236→                    "object": {"name": "Grunfeld", "type": "Dataset"},
   237→                    "confidence": 0.95,
   238→                    "evidence": "demonstrate the Fixed Effects estimator using the classic Grunfeld"
   239→                },
   240→                {
   241→                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
   242→                    "predicate": "PRODUCES",
   243→                    "object": {"name": "r2_within", "type": "ResultMetric"},
   244→                    "confidence": 0.90,
   245→                    "evidence": "result.r2_within"
   246→                },
   247→                {
   248→                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
   249→                    "predicate": "SUPPORTS_SE",
   250→                    "object": {"name": "clustered", "type": "StandardError"},
   251→                    "confidence": 0.95,
   252→                    "evidence": "model.fit(cov_type=\"clustered\")"
   253→                },
   254→            ]
   255→        },
   256→    },
   257→]
   258→
   259→
   260→def build_system_prompt() -> str:
   261→    """Build the system prompt with ontology schema."""
   262→    entity_list = "\n".join(f"- {et}" for et in ENTITY_TYPES)
   263→    relation_list = "\n".join(f"- {rt}" for rt in RELATION_TYPES)
   264→    return EXTRACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT.format(
   265→        entity_types=entity_list,
   266→        relation_types=relation_list,
   267→    )
   268→
   269→
   270→def build_user_prompt(content: str, chunk_type: str, source_file: str, context: str = "") -> str:
   271→    """Build the user prompt for a specific chunk."""
   272→    return EXTRACTION_USER_TEMPLATE.format(
   273→        content=content,
   274→        chunk_type=chunk_type,
   275→        source_file=source_file,
   276→        context=context or "PanelBox econometrics library",
   277→    )
   278→
   279→
   280→def build_few_shot_messages() -> list[dict]:
   281→    """Build few-shot example messages for the extraction prompt.
   282→
   283→    Returns list of message dicts with role/content for few-shot examples.
   284→    """
   285→    import json
   286→
   287→    messages = []
   288→    for example in FEW_SHOT_EXAMPLES:
   289→        # User message with example input
   290→        messages.append({
   291→            "role": "user",
   292→            "content": build_user_prompt(
   293→                content=example["content"],
   294→                chunk_type=example["chunk_type"],
   295→                source_file=f"example_{example['chunk_type']}.py",
   296→            ),
   297→        })
   298→        # Assistant message with expected output
   299→        messages.append({
   300→            "role": "assistant",
   301→            "content": json.dumps(example["expected_output"], indent=2),
   302→        })
   303→
   304→    return messages
   305→

"""Extraction prompt templates for triple extraction via Claude API.

Defines the system prompt with ontology constraints, user template
for chunk content, and few-shot examples for each source type.
"""

# --- Ontology Definition ---

ENTITY_TYPES = [
    "Estimator",
    "DiagnosticTest",
    "Parameter",
    "StatisticalConcept",
    "Literature",
    "ModelFamily",
    "StandardError",
    "Dataset",
    "CodePattern",
    "ResultMetric",
]

RELATION_TYPES = [
    "BELONGS_TO",
    "INHERITS_FROM",
    "VALIDATES",
    "REQUIRES",
    "PRODUCES",
    "ADDRESSES",
    "TESTS_FOR",
    "BASED_ON",
    "ALTERNATIVE_TO",
    "PRECONDITION",
    "SUPPORTS_SE",
    "DEMONSTRATED_IN",
    "IMPLEMENTED_BY",
    "SUCCEEDS",
]

# --- System Prompt ---

EXTRACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a knowledge graph extraction engine for the PanelBox econometrics library.

Your task is to extract (subject, predicate, object) triples from text chunks.
Each triple represents a relationship between two entities in the PanelBox domain.

## Valid Entity Types
{entity_types}

## Valid Relation Types
{relation_types}

## Rules
1. Only use entity types and relation types from the lists above.
2. Entity names should be PascalCase for classes (e.g., FixedEffects) and snake_case for parameters/concepts.
3. Each triple must have a confidence score between 0.0 and 1.0.
4. Include evidence: a short quote or reference from the source text.
5. Do NOT invent entities that are not mentioned or clearly implied in the text.
6. Prefer specific entity names over generic ones (e.g., "FixedEffects" not "model").
7. A single chunk may produce 0 to 15 triples.

## Output Format
Respond with a JSON object containing a "triples" array. Each triple has:
- subject: {{"name": str, "type": str}}
- predicate: str (one of the valid relation types)
- object: {{"name": str, "type": str}}
- confidence: float (0.0 to 1.0)
- evidence: str (short excerpt from source)

If no triples can be extracted, return {{"triples": []}}.
""".strip()

# --- User Template ---

EXTRACTION_USER_TEMPLATE = """Extract knowledge triples from this {chunk_type} chunk.

Source: {source_file}
Context: {context}

---
{content}
---

Return a JSON object with a "triples" array. Only use valid entity and relation types.""".strip()

# --- Few-Shot Examples ---

FEW_SHOT_EXAMPLES = [
    {
        "description": "Python class definition",
        "chunk_type": "python_class",
        "content": '''class FixedEffects(PanelEstimator):
    """Fixed Effects (Within) estimator for panel data.

    Uses the within transformation to eliminate entity-specific
    fixed effects. Supports robust and clustered standard errors.

    Based on Wooldridge (2002), Chapter 10.
    """

    def __init__(self, formula, data, entity_col, time_col,
                 cov_type="robust"):
        super().__init__(formula, data, entity_col, time_col)
        self.cov_type = cov_type

    def fit(self):
        """Fit the model using within transformation."""
        ...''',
        "expected_output": {
            "triples": [
                {
                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
                    "predicate": "BELONGS_TO",
                    "object": {"name": "StaticModels", "type": "ModelFamily"},
                    "confidence": 0.90,
                    "evidence": "class FixedEffects(PanelEstimator)"
                },
                {
                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
                    "predicate": "INHERITS_FROM",
                    "object": {"name": "PanelEstimator", "type": "Estimator"},
                    "confidence": 0.99,
                    "evidence": "class FixedEffects(PanelEstimator)"
                },
                {
                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
                    "predicate": "SUPPORTS_SE",
                    "object": {"name": "robust", "type": "StandardError"},
                    "confidence": 0.95,
                    "evidence": "Supports robust and clustered standard errors"
                },
                {
                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
                    "predicate": "SUPPORTS_SE",
                    "object": {"name": "clustered", "type": "StandardError"},
                    "confidence": 0.95,
                    "evidence": "Supports robust and clustered standard errors"
                },
                {
                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
                    "predicate": "BASED_ON",
                    "object": {"name": "Wooldridge2002", "type": "Literature"},
                    "confidence": 0.95,
                    "evidence": "Based on Wooldridge (2002), Chapter 10"
                },
                {
                    "subject": {"name": "FixedEffects", "type": "Estimator"},
                    "predicate": "ADDRESSES",
                    "object": {"name": "entity_fixed_effects", "type": "StatisticalConcept"},
                    "confidence": 0.90,
                    "evidence": "eliminate entity-specific fixed effects"
                },
            ]
        },
    },
    {
        "description": "Markdown documentation section",
        "chunk_type": "markdown_section",
        "content": '''## Hausman Test

The Hausman test compares Fixed Effects and Random Effects estimators
to determine whether the individual effects are correlated with the
regressors. Under the null hypothesis (RE is consistent), both
estimators are consistent, but FE is inefficient.

### Usage

```python
from panelbox.validation import hausman_test
result = hausman_test(fe_result, re_result)
print(result.statistic, result.p_value)
```

If p-value < 0.05, prefer Fixed Effects.''',
        "expected_output": {

                    "confidence": 0.95,

                },
                {

def build_system_prompt() -> str:
    """Build the system prompt with ontology schema."""
    entity_list = "\n".join(f"- {et}" for et in ENTITY_TYPES)
    relation_list = "\n".join(f"- {rt}" for rt in RELATION_TYPES)
    return EXTRACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT.format(
        entity_types=entity_list,
        relation_types=relation_list,
    )


def build_user_prompt(content: str, chunk_type: str, source_file: str, context: str = "") -> str:
    """Build the user prompt for a specific chunk."""
    return EXTRACTION_USER_TEMPLATE.format(
        content=content,
        chunk_type=chunk_type,
        source_file=source_file,
        context=context or "PanelBox econometrics library",
    )


def build_few_shot_messages() -> list[dict]:
    """Build few-shot example messages for the extraction prompt.

    Returns list of message dicts with role/content for few-shot examples.
    """
    import json

    messages = []
    for example in FEW_SHOT_EXAMPLES:
        # User message with example input
        messages.append({
            "role": "user",
            "content": build_user_prompt(
                content=example["content"],
                chunk_type=example["chunk_type"],
                source_file=f"example_{example['chunk_type']}.py",
            ),
        })
        # Assistant message with expected output
        messages.append({
            "role": "assistant",
            "content": json.dumps(example["expected_output"], indent=2),
        })

    return messages

# Subfase 01 - Ontologia Financeira, Corpus e Knowledge Graph

**Status**: PENDENTE
**Data**: 2026-03-07
**Dependencias**: Nenhuma
**Bloqueia**: SUBFASE_02, SUBFASE_03

## Objetivo

Adaptar a ontologia do PanelBox GraphRAG para o dominio financeiro, curar corpus regulatorio, processar documentos e construir knowledge graph financeiro com >500 triplas validadas.

## Descricao Tecnica

**Implementacao de referencia**: `/home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/graphrag/`

**Ontologia atual** (PanelBox):
- Arquivo: `/home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/graphrag/ontology/panelbox_ontology.yaml`
- 10 entity types: Estimator, DiagnosticTest, Parameter, StatisticalConcept, Literature, ModelFamily, StandardError, Dataset, CodePattern, ResultMetric
- 14 relationship types: BELONGS_TO, INHERITS_FROM, VALIDATES, REQUIRES, PRODUCES, ADDRESSES, TESTS_FOR, BASED_ON, ALTERNATIVE_TO, PRECONDITION, SUPPORTS_SE, DEMONSTRATED_IN, IMPLEMENTED_BY, SUCCEEDS

**Pipeline existente**:
- Chunker: `src/ingestion/chunker.py` (Python/Markdown/Notebook parsers)
- Triple Extractor: `src/ingestion/triple_extractor.py` (Claude API + Batch API)
- Entity Resolver: `src/ingestion/entity_resolver.py` (alias + fuzzy matching, threshold=85%)
- Graph Loader: `src/graph/graph_loader.py` (Cypher MERGE -> Neo4j)
- Prompts: `src/ingestion/prompts.py` (ontology-constrained extraction)

**Corpus a curar**:
- Basel III Framework (bis.org — publico)
- LGPD Lei 13.709/2018 (planalto.gov.br — publico)
- Resolucoes BCB selecionadas (bcb.gov.br — publico)
- SEC 10-K filings (EDGAR — publico, subconjunto 10-20 empresas)

**Arquivos a criar**:
- `/home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/papers/17_GraphRAG/implementacao/ontology/financial_ontology.yaml`
- `/home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/papers/17_GraphRAG/implementacao/ontology/financial_aliases.yaml`
- `/home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/papers/17_GraphRAG/implementacao/data/regulations/*.md`
- `/home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/papers/17_GraphRAG/implementacao/data/filings/*.md`

## INSTRUCAO CRITICA

**ANTES de criar qualquer arquivo**, leia a ontologia existente:
```bash
cat /home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/graphrag/ontology/panelbox_ontology.yaml
cat /home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/graphrag/ontology/aliases.yaml
cat /home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/graphrag/src/ingestion/prompts.py
```

Entenda o formato EXATO para replicar na ontologia financeira.

### Etapa 1: Definir ontologia financeira

Criar `financial_ontology.yaml` com entity types financeiros:
- Regulation, Article, Requirement, Obligation (regulatorios)
- Company, Subsidiary, FinancialMetric, RiskCategory (financeiros)
- AuditFinding, Control, Recommendation (auditoria)
- Jurisdiction, Regulator (jurisdicao)

E relationship types:
- REGULATES, REQUIRES, DEFINES, EXPOSES_TO, SUBSIDIARY_OF
- OPERATES_IN, AUDITED_BY, MITIGATES, REFERENCES, SUPERSEDES
- COMPOSED_OF, REPORTS_TO, VALIDATED_BY, COMPLIES_WITH

### Etapa 2: Criar aliases financeiros

Criar `financial_aliases.yaml` com pelo menos 50 aliases mapeando:
- "Basel III" -> BaselIII, "basileia iii" -> BaselIII
- "LGPD" -> LGPD, "lei geral de protecao de dados" -> LGPD
- Nomes de empresas, reguladores, metricas financeiras
- Bilingual (PT/EN)

### Etapa 3: Curar corpus regulatorio

Converter documentos regulatorios para Markdown:
- Basel III Pilar 1, 2, 3 (resumos estruturados)
- LGPD texto integral (65 artigos)
- Resolucoes BCB 4.893/2021 e 4.658/2018

Formato alvo: Markdown com headers H2/H3, YAML front matter:
```yaml
---
title: "Basel III - Pilar 1"
source: "Bank for International Settlements"
year: 2023
type: regulation
jurisdiction: international
---
```

### Etapa 4: Processar corpus via pipeline

Executar pipeline existente com ontologia financeira:
1. Chunking dos documentos
2. Extracao de triplas (ajustar prompts.py para tipos financeiros)
3. Resolucao de entidades
4. Carregamento no Neo4j

### Etapa 5: Validar qualidade do KG

Amostrar 50 triplas e avaliar:
- Precision > 80%
- Entity type accuracy > 85%
- Documentar metricas

## Criterios de Aceite

- [ ] Ontologia financeira criada com >=12 entity types e >=14 relationship types
- [ ] Arquivo financial_aliases.yaml com >=50 aliases bilingual (PT/EN)
- [ ] Corpus Basel III (Pilar 1, 2, 3) convertido para Markdown
- [ ] Corpus LGPD (65 artigos) convertido para Markdown
- [ ] Resolucoes BCB convertidas para Markdown
- [ ] Pipeline de ingestao executado com ontologia financeira
- [ ] Knowledge graph no Neo4j com >=500 triplas
- [ ] Amostra de 50 triplas avaliada com precision >=80%
- [ ] Metricas do pipeline documentadas (chunks, triplas, entidades, custo)
- [ ] Teste informal com 10 queries financeiras executado e documentado

## Riscos Tecnicos

| Risco | Probabilidade | Impacto | Mitigacao |
|-------|---------------|---------|-----------|
| Qualidade de extracao baixa com ontologia nova | Media | Alto | Ajustar prompts few-shot com exemplos financeiros |
| Documentos regulatorios dificeis de converter | Baixa | Medio | Usar versoes resumidas se necessario |
| Entity resolution falha em entidades financeiras | Media | Medio | Expandir aliases e ajustar threshold |
| Pipeline nao aceita novos entity types | Baixa | Alto | Verificar validacao em prompts.py antes |

**End of Specification**

# =============================================================================
# GraphRAG Financial Domain - Ontology Definition
# =============================================================================
# This file defines the knowledge graph schema for financial regulation,
# compliance, and corporate governance domains.
# Adapted from PanelBox GraphRAG ontology for the financial sector.
# =============================================================================

version: "1.0"
name: "Financial GraphRAG Ontology"
description: "Knowledge graph schema for financial regulations, compliance, and corporate governance"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entity Types (Node Labels in Neo4j)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
entity_types:

  Regulation:
    description: "Legal or regulatory framework (e.g., Basel III, LGPD, SOX)"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Canonical regulation name (e.g., BaselIII, LGPD)"
      full_title:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Full official title of the regulation"
      year:
        type: integer
        required: false
        description: "Year of enactment or latest revision"
      jurisdiction:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Jurisdiction scope (e.g., Brazil, International, USA)"
      regulatory_body:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Issuing authority (e.g., BCBS, BCB, SEC)"
      effective_date:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Date regulation became effective"
      status:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Current status (active, superseded, proposed)"

  Article:
    description: "Specific article, section, or clause within a regulation"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Article identifier (e.g., LGPD_Art7, BaselIII_Pillar1)"
      number:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Article or section number"
      title:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Article title or heading"
      text_summary:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Summary of the article content"
      chapter:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Chapter or part the article belongs to"

  Requirement:
    description: "Specific regulatory requirement or standard"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Requirement identifier (e.g., MinCapitalRatio, DataRetention)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "What the requirement mandates"
      threshold:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Quantitative threshold if applicable (e.g., 8% CAR)"
      deadline:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Compliance deadline if applicable"
      penalty:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Penalty for non-compliance"

  Obligation:
    description: "Duty or obligation imposed on regulated entities"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Obligation name (e.g., DataBreachNotification, AuditReport)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "What the obligation entails"
      frequency:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Frequency of obligation (annual, quarterly, event-driven)"
      responsible_party:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Who must fulfill the obligation"

  Company:
    description: "Financial institution or regulated company"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Company name (e.g., JPMorgan, BancoDoBrasil)"
      ticker:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Stock ticker symbol"
      sector:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Industry sector (e.g., Banking, Insurance, Asset Management)"
      country:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Country of incorporation"
      total_assets:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Total assets (latest reporting period)"
      systemically_important:
        type: boolean
        required: false
        description: "Whether designated as systemically important (G-SIB/D-SIB)"

  Subsidiary:
    description: "Subsidiary or business unit of a company"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Subsidiary name"
      parent_company:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Parent company name"
      country:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Country of operation"
      business_line:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Business line (e.g., retail banking, investment banking)"

  FinancialMetric:
    description: "Quantitative financial or risk metric"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Metric name (e.g., CET1Ratio, LCR, NPLRatio)"
      full_name:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Full metric name"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "What the metric measures"
      formula:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Calculation formula"
      regulatory_minimum:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Regulatory minimum threshold if applicable"
      unit:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Unit of measurement (%, bps, USD)"

  RiskCategory:
    description: "Category of financial or operational risk"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Risk category name (e.g., CreditRisk, MarketRisk, OperationalRisk)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Description of the risk type"
      measurement_approach:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Standard measurement approach (e.g., SA, IRB, AMA)"
      capital_charge:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Capital charge methodology"

  AuditFinding:
    description: "Finding from internal or external audit"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Finding identifier"
      severity:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Severity level (critical, high, medium, low)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Description of the finding"
      remediation_status:
        type: string
        required: false

# =============================================================================
# GraphRAG Financial Domain - Ontology Definition
# =============================================================================
# This file defines the knowledge graph schema for financial regulation,
# compliance, and corporate governance domains.
# Adapted from PanelBox GraphRAG ontology for the financial sector.
# =============================================================================

version: "1.0"
name: "Financial GraphRAG Ontology"
description: "Knowledge graph schema for financial regulations, compliance, and corporate governance"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entity Types (Node Labels in Neo4j)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
entity_types:

  Regulation:
    description: "Legal or regulatory framework (e.g., Basel III, LGPD, SOX)"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Canonical regulation name (e.g., BaselIII, LGPD)"
      full_title:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Full official title of the regulation"
      year:
        type: integer
        required: false
        description: "Year of enactment or latest revision"
      jurisdiction:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Jurisdiction scope (e.g., Brazil, International, USA)"
      regulatory_body:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Issuing authority (e.g., BCBS, BCB, SEC)"
      effective_date:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Date regulation became effective"
      status:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Current status (active, superseded, proposed)"

  Article:
    description: "Specific article, section, or clause within a regulation"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Article identifier (e.g., LGPD_Art7, BaselIII_Pillar1)"
      number:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Article or section number"
      title:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Article title or heading"
      text_summary:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Summary of the article content"
      chapter:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Chapter or part the article belongs to"

  Requirement:
    description: "Specific regulatory requirement or standard"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Requirement identifier (e.g., MinCapitalRatio, DataRetention)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "What the requirement mandates"
      threshold:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Quantitative threshold if applicable (e.g., 8% CAR)"
      deadline:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Compliance deadline if applicable"
      penalty:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Penalty for non-compliance"

  Obligation:
    description: "Duty or obligation imposed on regulated entities"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Obligation name (e.g., DataBreachNotification, AuditReport)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "What the obligation entails"
      frequency:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Frequency of obligation (annual, quarterly, event-driven)"
      responsible_party:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Who must fulfill the obligation"

  Company:
    description: "Financial institution or regulated company"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Company name (e.g., JPMorgan, BancoDoBrasil)"
      ticker:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Stock ticker symbol"
      sector:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Industry sector (e.g., Banking, Insurance, Asset Management)"
      country:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Country of incorporation"
      total_assets:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Total assets (latest reporting period)"
      systemically_important:
        type: boolean
        required: false
        description: "Whether designated as systemically important (G-SIB/D-SIB)"

  Subsidiary:
    description: "Subsidiary or business unit of a company"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Subsidiary name"
      parent_company:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Parent company name"
      country:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Country of operation"
      business_line:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Business line (e.g., retail banking, investment banking)"

  FinancialMetric:
    description: "Quantitative financial or risk metric"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Metric name (e.g., CET1Ratio, LCR, NPLRatio)"
      full_name:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Full metric name"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "What the metric measures"
      formula:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Calculation formula"
      regulatory_minimum:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Regulatory minimum threshold if applicable"
      unit:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Unit of measurement (%, bps, USD)"

  RiskCategory:
    description: "Category of financial or operational risk"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Risk category name (e.g., CreditRisk, MarketRisk, OperationalRisk)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Description of the risk type"
      measurement_approach:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Standard measurement approach (e.g., SA, IRB, AMA)"
      capital_charge:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Capital charge methodology"

  AuditFinding:
    description: "Finding from internal or external audit"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Finding identifier"
      severity:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Severity level (critical, high, medium, low)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Description of the finding"
      remediation_status:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Current remediation status"

  Control:
    description: "Internal control or compliance mechanism"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Control name (e.g., KYC, AML_Screening, DataEncryption)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "What the control does"
      control_type:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Type (preventive, detective, corrective)"
      automation_level:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Automation level (manual, semi-automated, fully automated)"
      testing_frequency:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "How often the control is tested"

  Recommendation:
    description: "Recommendation from audit, regulator, or best practice"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Recommendation identifier"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "What is recommended"
      source:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Source of recommendation (regulator, auditor, industry body)"
      priority:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Implementation priority (high, medium, low)"

  Jurisdiction:
    description: "Geographic or regulatory jurisdiction"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Jurisdiction name (e.g., Brazil, EU, USA)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Description of the jurisdiction scope"
      legal_system:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Legal system type (civil law, common law)"

  Regulator:
    description: "Regulatory body or supervisory authority"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Regulator name (e.g., BCB, SEC, ECB, BCBS)"
      full_name:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "Full official name"
      country:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Country or region"
      scope:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Regulatory scope (banking, securities, insurance, data protection)"

  DataCategory:
    description: "Category of data subject to regulation (personal, financial, sensitive)"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Data category name (e.g., PersonalData, SensitiveData, FinancialRecords)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "What data falls in this category"
      protection_level:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Required protection level"
      retention_period:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Regulatory retention period"

  ComplianceProcess:
    description: "Process or procedure for regulatory compliance"
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
        unique: true
        description: "Process name (e.g., KYC, AML, StressTesting)"
      description:
        type: string
        required: true
        description: "What the process involves"
      frequency:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Execution frequency"
      responsible_function:
        type: string
        required: false
        description: "Organizational function responsible"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Relationship Types (Edge Types in Neo4j)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
relationship_types:

  REGULATES:
    description: "Regulator or regulation governs an entity or activity"
    source: [Regulation, Regulator]

  REQUIRES:

  EXPOSES_TO:

  SUBSIDIARY_OF:

  OPERATES_IN:

# =============================================================================
# GraphRAG Financial Domain - Entity Aliases
# =============================================================================
# Maps common names, abbreviations, and variations to canonical entity names.
# Bilingual (Portuguese/English) for Brazilian financial regulation context.
# Used during entity resolution in the ingestion pipeline.
# =============================================================================

version: "1.0"

regulation_aliases:
  # Basel III / Basileia III
  "Basel III": "BaselIII"
  "Basel 3": "BaselIII"
  "Basileia III": "BaselIII"
  "Basileia 3": "BaselIII"
  "Basel III Framework": "BaselIII"
  "Acordo de Basileia": "BaselIII"
  "Basel Accord": "BaselIII"
  # LGPD
  "LGPD": "LGPD"
  "Lei Geral de Protecao de Dados": "LGPD"
  "Lei 13.709": "LGPD"
  "Lei 13709": "LGPD"
  "Lei 13.709/2018": "LGPD"
  "Brazilian Data Protection Law": "LGPD"
  "General Data Protection Law": "LGPD"
  # GDPR
  "GDPR": "GDPR"
  "General Data Protection Regulation": "GDPR"
  "Regulamento Geral de Protecao de Dados": "GDPR"
  # SOX
  "SOX": "SarbanesOxley"
  "Sarbanes-Oxley": "SarbanesOxley"
  "Sarbanes Oxley": "SarbanesOxley"
  "Sarbanes-Oxley Act": "SarbanesOxley"
  # Dodd-Frank
  "Dodd-Frank": "DoddFrank"
  "Dodd Frank": "DoddFrank"
  "Dodd-Frank Act": "DoddFrank"
  # MiFID II
  "MiFID II": "MiFIDII"
  "MiFID 2": "MiFIDII"
  "Markets in Financial Instruments Directive": "MiFIDII"
  # BCB Resolutions
  "Resolucao 4.893": "ResBCB4893"
  "Resolucao BCB 4893": "ResBCB4893"
  "Resolucao 4893/2021": "ResBCB4893"
  "Resolution 4893": "ResBCB4893"
  "Resolucao 4.658": "ResBCB4658"
  "Resolucao BCB 4658": "ResBCB4658"
  "Resolucao 4658/2018": "ResBCB4658"
  "Resolution 4658": "ResBCB4658"

regulator_aliases:
  # Banco Central do Brasil
  "BCB": "BancoCentralBrasil"
  "Bacen": "BancoCentralBrasil"
  "Banco Central": "BancoCentralBrasil"
  "Banco Central do Brasil": "BancoCentralBrasil"
  "Central Bank of Brazil": "BancoCentralBrasil"
  # BCBS
  "BCBS": "BaselCommittee"
  "Basel Committee": "BaselCommittee"
  "Comite de Basileia": "BaselCommittee"
  "Basel Committee on Banking Supervision": "BaselCommittee"
  # SEC
  "SEC": "SEC"
  "Securities and Exchange Commission": "SEC"
  "Comissao de Valores Mobiliarios americana": "SEC"
  # CVM
  "CVM": "CVM"
  "Comissao de Valores Mobiliarios": "CVM"
  "Brazilian Securities Commission": "CVM"
  # ECB
  "ECB": "ECB"
  "European Central Bank": "ECB"
  "Banco Central Europeu": "ECB"
  "BCE": "ECB"
  # ANPD
  "ANPD": "ANPD"
  "Autoridade Nacional de Protecao de Dados": "ANPD"
  "National Data Protection Authority": "ANPD"
  # Federal Reserve
  "Fed": "FederalReserve"
  "Federal Reserve": "FederalReserve"
  "Federal Reserve System": "FederalReserve"
  "Reserva Federal": "FederalReserve"

company_aliases:
  # Brazilian Banks
  "Banco do Brasil": "BancoDoBrasil"
  "BB": "BancoDoBrasil"
  "Itau": "ItauUnibanco"
  "Itau Unibanco": "ItauUnibanco"
  "Bradesco": "Bradesco"
  "Banco Bradesco": "Bradesco"

  "Santander BR": "SantanderBrasil"
  "BTG Pactual": "BTGPactual"
  "BTG": "BTGPactual"
  # US Banks

  "JPMorgan Chase": "JPMorgan"
  "Goldman Sachs": "GoldmanSachs"
  "Goldman": "GoldmanSachs"
  "Bank of America": "BankOfAmerica"
  "BofA": "BankOfAmerica"
  "Citigroup": "Citigroup"
  "Citi": "Citigroup"
  "Morgan Stanley": "MorganStanley"
  "Wells Fargo": "WellsFargo"

financial_metric_aliases:
  # Capital Ratios
  "CET1": "CET1Ratio"
  "CET1 Ratio": "CET1Ratio"
  "Common Equity Tier 1": "CET1Ratio"
  "Capital Proprio de Nivel 1": "CET1Ratio"
  "CAR": "CapitalAdequacyRatio"
  "Capital Adequacy Ratio": "CapitalAdequacyRatio"
  "Indice de Adequacao de Capital": "CapitalAdequacyRatio"
  "Indice de Basileia": "CapitalAdequacyRatio"
  "Tier 1": "Tier1Capital"

  # Liquidity
  "LCR": "LCR"

  "Indice de Cobertura de Liquidez": "LCR"
  "NSFR": "NSFR"
  "Net Stable Funding Ratio": "NSFR"
  "Indice de Financiamento Estavel": "NSFR"
  # Credit Risk
  "NPL": "NPLRatio"
  "NPL Ratio": "NPLRatio"
  "Non-Performing Loans": "NPLRatio"
  "Inadimplencia": "NPLRatio"
  "PD": "ProbabilityOfDefault"
  "Probability of Default": "ProbabilityOfDefault"
  "Probabilidade de Default": "ProbabilityOfDefault"
  "LGD": "LossGivenDefault"
  "Loss Given Default": "LossGivenDefault"
  "Perda Dado o Default": "LossGivenDefault"
  "EAD": "ExposureAtDefault"
  "Exposure at Default": "ExposureAtDefault"
  "Exposicao no Momento do Default": "ExposureAtDefault"
  # Market Risk
  "VaR": "ValueAtRisk"
  "Value at Risk": "ValueAtRisk"
  "Valor em Risco": "ValueAtRisk"
  "ES": "ExpectedShortfall"
  "Expected Shortfall": "ExpectedShortfall"
  "CVaR": "ExpectedShortfall"
  "Conditional VaR": "ExpectedShortfall"
  # Leverage
  "Leverage Ratio": "LeverageRatio"
  "Indice de Alavancagem": "LeverageRatio"
  "RWA": "RiskWeightedAssets"
  "Risk-Weighted Assets": "RiskWeightedAssets"

tool_use_result.originalFile:
# Subfase 01 - Ontologia Financeira, Corpus e Knowledge Graph

**Status**: PENDENTE
**Data**: 2026-03-07
**Dependencias**: Nenhuma
**Bloqueia**: SUBFASE_02, SUBFASE_03

## Objetivo

Adaptar a ontologia do PanelBox GraphRAG para o dominio financeiro, curar corpus regulatorio, processar documentos e construir knowledge graph financeiro com >500 triplas validadas.

## Descricao Tecnica

**Implementacao de referencia**: `/home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/graphrag/`

**Ontologia atual** (PanelBox):
- Arquivo: `/home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/graphrag/ontology/panelbox_ontology.yaml`
- 10 entity types: Estimator, DiagnosticTest, Parameter, StatisticalConcept, Literature, ModelFamily, StandardError, Dataset, CodePattern, ResultMetric
- 14 relationship types: BELONGS_TO, INHERITS_FROM, VALIDATES, REQUIRES, PRODUCES, ADDRESSES, TESTS_FOR, BASED_ON, ALTERNATIVE_TO, PRECONDITION, SUPPORTS_SE, DEMONSTRATED_IN, IMPLEMENTED_BY, SUCCEEDS

**Pipeline existente**:
- Chunker: `src/ingestion/chunker.py` (Python/Markdown/Notebook parsers)
- Triple Extractor: `src/ingestion/triple_extractor.py` (Claude API + Batch API)
- Entity Resolver: `src/ingestion/entity_resolver.py` (alias + fuzzy matching, threshold=85%)
- Graph Loader: `src/graph/graph_loader.py` (Cypher MERGE -> Neo4j)
- Prompts: `src/ingestion/prompts.py` (ontology-constrained extraction)

**Corpus a curar**:
- Basel III Framework (bis.org — publico)
- LGPD Lei 13.709/2018 (planalto.gov.br — publico)
- Resolucoes BCB selecionadas (bcb.gov.br — publico)
- SEC 10-K filings (EDGAR — publico, subconjunto 10-20 empresas)

**Arquivos a criar**:
- `/home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/papers/17_GraphRAG/implementacao/ontology/financial_ontology.yaml`
- `/home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/papers/17_GraphRAG/implementacao/ontology/financial_aliases.yaml`
- `/home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/papers/17_GraphRAG/implementacao/data/regulations/*.md`
- `/home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/papers/17_GraphRAG/implementacao/data/filings/*.md`

## INSTRUCAO CRITICA

**ANTES de criar qualquer arquivo**, leia a ontologia existente:
```bash
cat /home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/graphrag/ontology/panelbox_ontology.yaml
cat /home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/graphrag/ontology/aliases.yaml
cat /home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/graphrag/src/ingestion/prompts.py
```

Entenda o formato EXATO para replicar na ontologia financeira.

### Etapa 1: Definir ontologia financeira

Criar `financial_ontology.yaml` com entity types financeiros:
- Regulation, Article, Requirement, Obligation (regulatorios)
- Company, Subsidiary, FinancialMetric, RiskCategory (financeiros)
- AuditFinding, Control, Recommendation (auditoria)
- Jurisdiction, Regulator (jurisdicao)

E relationship types:
- REGULATES, REQUIRES, DEFINES, EXPOSES_TO, SUBSIDIARY_OF
- OPERATES_IN, AUDITED_BY, MITIGATES, REFERENCES, SUPERSEDES
- COMPOSED_OF, REPORTS_TO, VALIDATED_BY, COMPLIES_WITH

### Etapa 2: Criar aliases financeiros

Criar `financial_aliases.yaml` com pelo menos 50 aliases mapeando:
- "Basel III" -> BaselIII, "basileia iii" -> BaselIII
- "LGPD" -> LGPD, "lei geral de protecao de dados" -> LGPD
- Nomes de empresas, reguladores, metricas financeiras
- Bilingual (PT/EN)

### Etapa 3: Curar corpus regulatorio

Converter documentos regulatorios para Markdown:
- Basel III Pilar 1, 2, 3 (resumos estruturados)
- LGPD texto integral (65 artigos)
- Resolucoes BCB 4.893/2021 e 4.658/2018

Formato alvo: Markdown com headers H2/H3, YAML front matter:
```yaml
---
title: "Basel III - Pilar 1"
source: "Bank for International Settlements"
year: 2023
type: regulation
jurisdiction: international
---
```

### Etapa 4: Processar corpus via pipeline

Executar pipeline existente com ontologia financeira:
1. Chunking dos documentos
2. Extracao de triplas (ajustar prompts.py para tipos financeiros)
3. Resolucao de entidades
4. Carregamento no Neo4j

### Etapa 5: Validar qualidade do KG

Amostrar 50 triplas e avaliar:
- Precision > 80%
- Entity type accuracy > 85%
- Documentar metricas

## Criterios de Aceite

- [ ] Ontologia financeira criada com >=12 entity types e >=14 relationship types
- [ ] Arquivo financial_aliases.yaml com >=50 aliases bilingual (PT/EN)
- [ ] Corpus Basel III (Pilar 1, 2, 3) convertido para Markdown
- [ ] Corpus LGPD (65 artigos) convertido para Markdown
- [ ] Resolucoes BCB convertidas para Markdown
- [ ] Pipeline de ingestao executado com ontologia financeira
- [ ] Knowledge graph no Neo4j com >=500 triplas
- [ ] Amostra de 50 triplas avaliada com precision >=80%
- [ ] Metricas do pipeline documentadas (chunks, triplas, entidades, custo)
- [ ] Teste informal com 10 queries financeiras executado e documentado

## Riscos Tecnicos

| Risco | Probabilidade | Impacto | Mitigacao |
|-------|---------------|---------|-----------|
| Qualidade de extracao baixa com ontologia nova | Media | Alto | Ajustar prompts few-shot com exemplos financeiros |
| Documentos regulatorios dificeis de converter | Baixa | Medio | Usar versoes resumidas se necessario |
| Entity resolution falha em entidades financeiras | Media | Medio | Expandir aliases e ajustar threshold |
| Pipeline nao aceita novos entity types | Baixa | Alto | Verificar validacao em prompts.py antes |

**End of Specification**

content.text: Now I'll create the three Basel III pillar documents, the LGPD corpus, and the BCB resolutions in parallel using subagents, since these are large content files.

input.prompt:
Create a structured Markdown file at `/home/guhaase/projetos/panelbox/papers/17_GraphRAG/implementacao/data/regulations/basel3_pillar1.md` for Basel III Pillar 1 (Minimum Capital Requirements).

The file must follow this EXACT format with YAML front matter:

```yaml
---
title: "Basel III - Pillar 1: Minimum Capital Requirements"
source: "Bank for International Settlements"
year: 2023
type: regulation
jurisdiction: international
regulatory_body: "Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS)"
document_id: "BaselIII_Pillar1"
---
```

Content requirements:
- Write in ENGLISH
- Use H2 (##) for major sections, H3 (###) for subsections
- Cover ALL key aspects of Basel III Pillar 1 thoroughly so that knowledge graph triples can be extracted
- Must include these sections at minimum:
  1. Overview (purpose, history, Basel I -> II -> III evolution)
  2. Capital Definition (CET1, AT1, Tier 2, deductions)
  3. Minimum Capital Requirements (4.5% CET1, 6% Tier 1, 8% Total)
  4. Capital Buffers (Conservation buffer 2.5%, Countercyclical 0-2.5%, G-SIB/D-SIB surcharges)
  5. Credit Risk - Standardised Approach (risk weights by asset class, external ratings)
  6. Credit Risk - Internal Ratings-Based (IRB) Approach (Foundation IRB, Advanced IRB, PD/LGD/EAD formulas)
  7. Market Risk (Fundamental Review of the Trading Book - FRTB, Standardised Approach, Internal Models Approach)
  8. Operational Risk (Standardised Measurement Approach - SMA, replacing AMA)
  9. Leverage Ratio (3% minimum, calculation methodology)
  10. Output Floor (72.5% of standardised approaches)
  11. Risk-Weighted Assets (RWA calculation overview)
  12. Counterparty Credit Risk (SA-CCR)

- Include specific numerical thresholds, percentages, and formulas where relevant
- Reference specific BCBS publications (e.g., BCBS d424, BCBS d457)
- Each section should have 3-8 paragraphs to be rich enough for triple extraction
- Target: ~2000-2500 words total
- Do NOT use emojis

```yaml
---
```
```yaml
title: "Basel III - Pillar 2: Supervisory Review Process"
source: "Bank for International Settlements"
year: 2023
type: regulation
jurisdiction: international
regulatory_body: "Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS)"
document_id: "BaselIII_Pillar2"
---
```

Content requirements:
- Write in ENGLISH
- Use H2 (##) for major sections, H3 (###) for subsections
- Cover ALL key aspects of Basel III Pillar 2 thoroughly for knowledge graph triple extraction

  6. Credit Concentration Risk
  7. Liquidity Risk Management
  8. Stress Testing Requirements
  9. Corporate Governance and Risk Management
  10. Pillar 2 Capital Add-Ons
  11. Supervisory Intervention and Corrective Actions

- Include specific BCBS guidance references
- Each section should have 3-6 paragraphs
- Target: ~1500-2000 words total
- Do NOT use emojis
